Now let's go to the next one which is the I care tonality or the I really want to know tonality. So it's when I say you know, hey, you know, John, how you doing today? Like I really want to know I care versus yeah, John, how you doing today? Yeah, John, how's it going today? John, how's it going today versus hey, John, how's it going today? And look at my hands together. Okay. It's not like John, how you going today? How you going today? And I really want to know and that creates that little bit of reciprocity and so on. They say, well, okay, nice person. He sounds like he cares. I'll listen further. Let me just put a few together to you right now into an opening into a phone call that you typically would make. Let's say you were going to speak to someone that you never spoke to before, right? You got a referral. So you knew their name and you know, had a connection. So you call up, you get through the secretary, you get the person on the phone and you say, hey, John, Jordan Belfort calling from sales, a Q in Australia. How you going today? And he's like, I'm okay. Pretty good. And he matches my tonality. Right. Okay. I'm okay. Pretty good versus I say, yeah, hey, it's Jordan calling from sales in Q in Australia. How you doing today, John? He's like, I'm all right. And immediately the whole conversation is framed differently because of the tonality I use. The words were the same. In one way, I have the guy right where I want him emotionally. Okay. I said, you know, hey, Jordan Belfort calling. He's like, who, what? And he thinks for a second, say, then I say, you know, how you going today? And he's like, I'm okay. Before he knows it, he's like, oh, I like this guy. Who he doesn't work against me. Well, she doesn't work against me in her internal dialogue. It's in conjunction. They're in that sort of that weird magnetic zone that's called unconscious rapport. See conscious rapport is more about the similarities and you know, you have some things in common with them. And actually, oh yeah, I like this person because of versus unconscious approaches. You know, I just kind of liked that person. There's something about them that's just likable. And as I said before, the eye care and being like a likability are two of the most critical elements of getting into rapport and maintaining rapport.
